projectName: Freelance Web and AI Studio Site
projectSummary: A first MVP marketing site for a freelance web and AI consultant that explains the offer, builds trust, and converts qualified visitors into contact requests.
assumptions: The first release should optimize for clarity and conversion rather than advanced product features.
The initial scope focuses on a marketing site with a small number of pages.
questions: Should the first release include booking availability or only a contact path?
Is an English version required in the initial MVP?

items.title: Clarify the freelance offer
items.description: Define the first-release messaging and key value proposition for target visitors.
notes: Keep the initial story set focused on the first release.

items.title: Present a clear homepage value proposition
items.description: As a target visitor, I want to understand the offer within a few seconds so I can decide whether to continue exploring the site.
acceptanceCriteria: The homepage states what service is offered and for whom.
A primary contact-oriented call to action is visible near the top of the page.
The messaging stays aligned with the first MVP scope.
assumptions: The homepage is the main entry point for new visitors.

items.title: Draft homepage content structure
items.description: Outline the homepage sections, message hierarchy, and user journey before implementation planning.

items.title: Prepare homepage implementation tasks
items.description: Split the homepage story into front-end implementation work that a developer can review next.

items.title: Scope drift toward non-MVP features
items.description: The backlog may expand toward CMS or advanced integrations unless the team reviews each addition against the MVP constraints.
notes: Review any new feature request against the brief before accepting it.
